items.headline: Royals starter Cole Ragans removed after 3 scoreless innings against Cleveland
items.description:
KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Kansas City Royals starter Cole Ragans was removed after three scoreless innings and 58 pitches against Cleveland on Wednesday night.
Luinder Avila started warming up in the bottom of the third and entered to start the fourth. There was no immediate announcement by the team of any injury.

items.headline: Misdemeanor battery charges against Dallas Wings star Arike Ogunbowale dropped in Miami, report says
items.description:
MIAMI (AP) — Misdemeanor battery charges have been dropped against Dallas Wings star Arike Ogunbowale, according to an ESPN report.
The network reported that the case against Ogunbowale was dropped by the Florida State Attorney’s Office during a hearing Wednesday morning.

items.headline: Roma's Champions League return back on as Milan, Juve wobble
items.description: Roma have a return to the Champions League firmly within their sights ahead of Sunday's trip to Parma as both Juventus and AC Milan's hope's of finishing in Serie A's top four...

items.headline: Second baseman Gleyber Torres put on 10-day injured list by Tigers
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Detroit Tigers second baseman Gleyber Torres was put on the 10-day injured list Wednesday because of a left oblique strain.
Detroit made the move retroactive to Monday,

items.headline: Reds closer Emilio Pagán placed on IL with left hamstring strain
items.description:
CHICAGO (AP) — Cincinnati Reds closer Emilio Pagán thought his season was over on Tuesday night.
A day later, it was a more positive timeline for the right-hander.

items.headline: Bucks co-owner Jimmy Haslam wants Giannis Antetokounmpo's future settled before the NBA draft
items.description:
MILWAUKEE (AP) — Milwaukee Bucks co-owner Jimmy Haslam says he would like to have Giannis Antetokounmpo's future sorted out before next month’s NBA draft.
Haslam spoke Wednesday alongside general manager Jon Horst at a news conference introducing Taylor Jenkins as the Bucks’ coach. Antetokounmpo has spent his entire 13-year career with the Bucks, but it's possible he could leave Milwaukee after a frustrating, injury-marred season.

items.headline: From cricket’s capital to Olympic ambitions, India’s next play on the world sports stage
items.description:
NEW DELHI (AP) — With a massive population, a rapidly growing economy and already the global powerbroker in cricket, India is preparing to expand its influence in international sport.
The 2030 Commonwealth Games are already locked in for Ahmedabad, and there's high expectations for India's bid to host the 2036 Olympics.

items.headline: Williamson named in New Zealand squad for Ireland, England Tests
items.description: Kane Williamson will continue his pursuit of 10,000 Test runs after being named Thursday in New Zealand's squad to play Ireland and...

items.headline: Patriots QB Drake Maye stands behind coach Mike Vrabel, calling him 'a great human being'
items.description:
CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — New England Patriots quarterback Drake Maye called Mike Vrabel “a great human being,” and said he doesn't think the head coach's off-field issues will be a distraction for the defending AFC champions this upcoming season.
“No, I don't," Maye said. "I mean, he’s our head coach. I think he’s done a great job of talking to us and talking us through it. I’m just looking forward to getting back to work and getting ready.”

items.headline: Hornets guard Brandon Miller out indefinitely after surgery to address left shoulder instability
items.description:
CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Charlotte Hornets guard Brandon Miller underwent surgery on Wednesday to address left shoulder instability.
Miller will be out indefinitely and additional updates on his status will be provided as appropriate, but the team said he is expected to make a full recovery.
